                {
                  "lineNumber": 127,
                  "content": "In order to set a status of &#96;paused&#96; for a source/sink, the storage controller will need to know the dataflow's backing replica at the time the replica is dropped. The mapping between observed sources/sinks currently exists in &#96;mz_storage_client::controller::rehydration::RehydrationTask&#96;, however we can lift it up into &#96;RehydrationStorageClient&#96; in order for the storage controller to access it without duplicating the data. Furthermore, to combat cases in which &#96;environmentd&#96; crashes before it can successfully write a &#96;paused&#96; status, the storage controller should write a status of &#96;paused&#96; for any dataflows that belong to an instance with 0 replicas immediately following coordinator initialization."
                }

        "contents": "    /// This flag should only be set if the process is provided with exclusive access to its\n    /// supplied CPU cores. If other processes are competing over the same cores, setting core\n    /// affinity might degrade dataflow performance rather than improving it.\n    #[clap(long)]\n    worker_core_affinity: bool,\n}\n\npub fn main() {\n    mz_ore::panic::install_enhanced_handler();\n\n    let args = cli::parse_args(CliConfig {\n        env_prefix: Some(\"CLUSTERD_\"),\n        enable_version_flag: true,\n    });\n\n    let ncpus_useful = usize::max(1, std::cmp::min(num_cpus::get(), num_cpus::get_physical()));\n    let runtime = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_multi_thread()\n        .worker_threads(ncpus_useful)\n        .thread_stack_size(3 * 1024 * 1024) // 3 MiB\n        // The default thread name exceeds the Linux limit on thread name\n        // length, so pick something shorter. The maximum length is 16 including\n        // a \\0 terminator. This gives us four decimals, which should be enough\n        // for most existing computers.\n        .thread_name_fn(|| {\n            use std::sync::atomic::{AtomicUsize, Ordering};\n            static ATOMIC_ID: AtomicUsize = AtomicUsize::new(0);\n            let id = ATOMIC_ID.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);\n            format!(\"tokio:work-{}\", id)\n        })\n        .enable_all()\n        .build()\n        .unwrap();\n    if let Err(err) = runtime.block_on(run(args)) {\n        panic!(\"clusterd: fatal: {}\", err.display_with_causes());\n    }\n}\n\nasync fn run(args: Args) -&gt; Result&lt;(), anyhow::Error&gt;",

        "contents": "def test_crash_environmentd(mz: MaterializeApplication) -&gt; None:\n    def restarts(p: V1Pod) -&gt; int:\n        assert p.status is not None\n        assert p.status.container_statuses is not None\n        return p.status.container_statuses[0].restart_count\n\n    def get_replica() -&gt; tuple[V1Pod, V1StatefulSet]:\n        \"\"\"Find the stateful set for the replica of the default cluster\"\"\"\n        compute_pod_name = \"cluster-u1-replica-u1-gen-0-0\"\n        ss_name = \"cluster-u1-replica-u1-gen-0\"\n        compute_pod = mz.environmentd.api().read_namespaced_pod(\n            compute_pod_name, mz.environmentd.namespace()\n        )\n        for ss in (\n            mz.environmentd.apps_api().list_stateful_set_for_all_namespaces().items\n        ):\n            assert ss.metadata is not None\n            if ss.metadata.name == ss_name:\n                return (compute_pod, ss)\n        raise RuntimeError(f\"No data found for {ss_name}\")\n\n    populate(mz, 2)\n\n    before = get_replica()\n\n    try:\n        mz.environmentd.sql(\"SELECT mz_unsafe.mz_panic('forced panic')\")\n    except InterfaceError:\n        pass\n    validate(mz, 2)\n\n    after = get_replica()\n\n    # A environmentd crash must not restart other nodes\n    assert restarts(before[0]) == restarts(after[0])\n\n\ndef test_crash_clusterd(mz: MaterializeApplication) -&gt; None:\n    populate(mz, 3)",

        "contents": "fn run(mut args: Args) -&gt; Result&lt;(), anyhow::Error&gt; {\n    mz_ore::panic::install_enhanced_handler();\n    let envd_start = Instant::now();\n\n    // Configure signal handling as soon as possible. We want signals to be\n    // handled to our liking ASAP.\n    sys::enable_sigusr2_coverage_dump()?;\n    sys::enable_termination_signal_cleanup()?;\n\n    let license_key = if let Some(license_key_file) = args.license_key {\n        let license_key_text = std::fs::read_to_string(&amp;license_key_file)\n            .context(\"failed to open license key file\")?;\n        let license_key = mz_license_keys::validate(license_key_text.trim())\n            .context(\"failed to validate license key file\")?;\n        if license_key.expired {\n            let message = format!(\n                \"The license key provided at {license_key_file} is expired! Please contact Materialize for assistance.\"\n            );\n            match license_key.expiration_behavior {\n                ExpirationBehavior::Warn | ExpirationBehavior::DisableClusterCreation =&gt; {\n                    warn!(\"{message}\");\n                }\n                ExpirationBehavior::Disable =&gt; bail!(\"{message}\"),\n            }\n        }\n        license_key\n    } else if matches!(args.orchestrator, OrchestratorKind::Kubernetes) {\n        bail!(\"--license-key is required when running in Kubernetes\");\n    } else {\n        // license key checks are optional for the emulator\n        ValidatedLicenseKey::disabled()\n    };\n\n    // Configure testing options.\n    let force_builtin_schema_migration = args\n        .unsafe_force_builtin_schema_migration\n        .inspect(|_mechanism| assert!(args.unsafe_mode));\n\n    // Start Tokio runtime.\n\n    let ncpus_useful = usize::max(1, cmp::min(num_cpus::get(), num_cpus::get_physical()));\n    let runtime = Arc::new(\n        tokio::runtime::Builder::new_multi_thread()",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./misc/python/materialize/cli/run.py",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 91
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 484
          }
        },
        "contents": "def main() -&gt; int:\n\n    assert child_pid &gt;= 0\n    if child_pid &gt; 0:\n        # This is the parent process, responsible for cleaning up after the\n        # child.\n\n        # First, arrange to terminate all processes in the child's process group\n        # when we exit.\n        def _kill_childpg():\n            try:\n                os.killpg(child_pid, signal.SIGTERM)\n            except ProcessLookupError:\n                pass\n\n        atexit.register(_kill_childpg)\n\n        # Wait for the child to exit then propagate its exit status.\n        _, ws = os.waitpid(child_pid, 0)\n        exit(os.waitstatus_to_exitcode(ws))\n\n    # This is the child. First, move to a dedicated process group.\n    os.setpgid(child_pid, child_pid)\n\n    # Then, spawn the desired command.\n    print(f\"$ {' '.join(command)}\")\n    if args.program == \"environmentd\":\n        # Automatically restart &#96;environmentd&#96; after it halts, but not more than\n        # once every 5s to prevent hot loops. This simulates what happens when\n        # running in Kubernetes, which restarts failed &#96;environmentd&#96; process\n        # automatically. (We don't restart after a panic, since panics are\n        # generally unexpected and we don't want to inadvertently hide them\n        # during local development.)\n        while True:\n            last_start_time = datetime.now()\n            proc = subprocess.run(command, env=env)\n            if proc.returncode == 166:\n                wait = max(\n                    timedelta(seconds=5) - (datetime.now() - last_start_time),\n                    timedelta(seconds=0),\n                )\n                print(f\"environmentd halted; will restart in {wait.total_seconds()}s\")\n                time.sleep(wait.total_seconds())\n            else:\n                break\n    else:\n        proc = subprocess.run(command, env=env)\n\n    exit(proc.returncode)\n\n\ndef _cargo_build(\n    args: argparse.Namespace, extra_programs: list[str] = []\n) -&gt; tuple[int, list[str]]:\n    ",

        "contents": "    /// should not be set in production.\n    #[clap(long, value_enum, requires = \"unsafe_mode\")]\n    unsafe_force_builtin_schema_migration: Option&lt;String&gt;,\n}\n\n#[derive(ValueEnum, Debug, Clone)]\nenum OrchestratorKind {\n    Kubernetes,\n    Process,\n}\n\n// TODO [Alex Hunt] move this to a shared function that can be imported by the\n// region-controller.\nfn aws_secrets_controller_prefix(env_id: &amp;EnvironmentId) -&gt; String {\n    format!(\"/user-managed/{}/\", env_id)\n}\nfn aws_secrets_controller_key_alias(env_id: &amp;EnvironmentId) -&gt; String {\n    // TODO [Alex Hunt] move this to a shared function that can be imported by the\n    // region-controller.\n    format!(\"alias/customer_key_{}\", env_id)\n}\n\npub fn main() {\n    let args = cli::parse_args(CliConfig {\n        env_prefix: Some(\"MZ_\"),\n        enable_version_flag: true,\n    });\n    if let Err(err) = run(args) {\n        panic!(\"environmentd: fatal: {}\", err.display_with_causes());\n    }\n}\n\nfn run(mut args: Args) -&gt; Result&lt;(), anyhow::Error&gt;",

        "contents": "def test_crash_clusterd(mz: MaterializeApplication) -&gt; None:\n    populate(mz, 3)\n    mz.testdrive.run(\n        input=dedent(\n            \"\"\"\n            $[version&gt;=5500] postgres-execute connection=postgres://mz_system:materialize@${testdrive.materialize-internal-sql-addr}\n            ALTER SYSTEM SET unsafe_enable_unstable_dependencies = true;\n            \"\"\"\n        ),\n        no_reset=True,\n    )\n    mz.environmentd.sql(\"CREATE TABLE crash_table (f1 TEXT)\")\n    mz.environmentd.sql(\n        \"CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW crash_view AS SELECT mz_unsafe.mz_panic(f1) FROM crash_table\"\n    )\n    mz.environmentd.sql(\"INSERT INTO crash_table VALUES ('forced panic')\")\n\n    mz.testdrive.run(\n        input=dedent(\n            \"\"\"\n            &gt; DROP MATERIALIZED VIEW crash_view\n            \"\"\"\n        ),\n        no_reset=True,\n        seed=3,\n    )\n\n    validate(mz, 3)",

        "contents": "(&amp;state_updater, &amp;pid_file).await;\n\n            loop {\n                let mut cmd = launch_spec.refine_command(\n                    &amp;image,\n                    &amp;args,\n                    &amp;command_wrapper,\n                    memory_limit.as_ref(),\n                    cpu_limit.as_ref(),\n                );\n                info!(\n                    \"launching {full_id}-{i} via {} {}...\",\n                    cmd.as_std().get_program().to_string_lossy(),\n                    cmd.as_std()\n                        .get_args()\n                        .map(|arg| arg.to_string_lossy())\n                        .join(\" \")\n                );\n                if suppress_output {\n                    cmd.stdout(Stdio::null());\n                    cmd.stderr(Stdio::null());\n                }\n                match spawn_process(&amp;state_updater, cmd, &amp;pid_file, !command_wrapper.is_empty())\n                    .await\n                {\n                    Ok(status) =&gt; {\n                        if propagate_crashes &amp;&amp; did_process_crash(status) {\n                            panic!(\n                                \"{full_id}-{i} crashed; aborting because propagate_crashes is enabled\"\n                            );\n                        }\n                        error!(\"{full_id}-{i} exited: {:?}; relaunching in 5s\", status);\n                    }\n                    Err(e) =&gt; {\n                        error!(\"{full_id}-{i} failed to spawn: {}; relaunching in 5s\", e);\n                    }\n                };\n                state_updater.update_state(ProcessStatus::NotReady);\n                time::sleep(Duration::from_secs(5)).await;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    async fn maybe_write_prometheus_service_discovery_file",

        "contents": "async fn run(args: Args) -&gt; Result&lt;(), anyhow::Error&gt; {\n    let metrics_registry = MetricsRegistry::new();\n    let tracing_handle = args\n        .tracing\n        .configure_tracing(\n            StaticTracingConfig {\n                service_name: \"clusterd\",\n                build_info: BUILD_INFO,\n            },\n            metrics_registry.clone(),\n        )\n        .await?;\n\n    let tracing_handle = Arc::new(tracing_handle);\n    register_runtime_metrics(\"main\", Handle::current().metrics(), &amp;metrics_registry);\n\n    // Keep this _after_ the mz_ore::tracing::configure call so that its panic\n    // hook runs _before_ the one that sends things to sentry.\n    mz_timely_util::panic::halt_on_timely_communication_panic();\n\n    let _failpoint_scenario = FailScenario::setup();\n\n    emit_boot_diagnostics!(&amp;BUILD_INFO);\n\n    mz_alloc::register_metrics_into(&amp;metrics_registry).await;\n    mz_metrics::register_metrics_into(&amp;metrics_registry, mz_dyncfgs::all_dyncfgs()).await;\n\n    if let Some(heap_limit) = args.heap_limit {\n        mz_compute::memory_limiter::start_limiter(heap_limit, &amp;metrics_registry);\n    } else {\n        info!(\"no heap limit announced; disabling memory limiter\");\n    }\n\n    let secrets_reader = args\n        .secrets\n        .load()\n        .await\n        .context(\"loading secrets reader\")?;\n\n    let usage_collector = Arc::new(usage_metrics::Collector {\n        disk_root: args.scratch_directory.clone(),\n    });\n\n    mz_ore::task::spawn(|| \"clusterd_internal_http_server\",",

        "contents": "def test_crash_storage(mz: MaterializeApplication) -&gt; None:\n    populate(mz, 1)\n\n    [cluster_id, replica_id] = mz.environmentd.sql_query(\n        \"SELECT s.cluster_id, r.id FROM mz_sources s JOIN mz_cluster_replicas r ON r.cluster_id = s.cluster_id WHERE s.name = 's1'\"\n    )[0]\n    pod_name = cluster_pod_name(cluster_id, replica_id)\n\n    wait(condition=\"jsonpath={.status.phase}=Running\", resource=pod_name)\n    try:\n        mz.kubectl(\"exec\", pod_name, \"--\", \"bash\", \"-c\", \"kill -9 &#96;pidof clusterd&#96;\")\n    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:\n        # Killing the entrypoint via kubectl may result in kubectl exiting with code 137\n        assert e.returncode == 137\n\n    wait(condition=\"jsonpath={.status.phase}=Running\", resource=pod_name)\n\n    validate(mz, 1)\n\n\ndef test_crash_environmentd(mz: MaterializeApplication) -&gt; None:\n    def restarts(p: V1Pod) -&gt; int:\n        assert p.status is not None",
        "signatures": {},

        "contents": "SERVICES = [\n    Zookeeper(),\n    Kafka(),\n    SchemaRegistry(),\n    Localstack(),\n    Clusterd(name=\"clusterd1\", workers=2),\n    Clusterd(name=\"clusterd2\", workers=2),\n    Clusterd(name=\"clusterd3\", workers=2),\n    Clusterd(name=\"clusterd4\", workers=2),\n    Mz(app_password=\"\"),\n    Minio(),\n    Materialized(\n        # We use mz_panic() in some test scenarios, so environmentd must stay up.\n        propagate_crashes=False,\n        external_metadata_store=True,\n        additional_system_parameter_defaults={\n            \"unsafe_enable_unsafe_functions\": \"true\",\n            \"unsafe_enable_unorchestrated_cluster_replicas\": \"true\",\n        },\n        support_external_clusterd=True,\n    ),\n    CockroachOrPostgresMetadata(),\n    Postgres(),\n    Redpanda(),\n    Toxiproxy(),\n    Testdrive(\n        volume_workdir=\"../testdrive:/workdir/testdrive\",\n        volumes_extra=[\".:/workdir/smoke\"],\n        materialize_params={\"cluster\": \"cluster1\"},\n    ),\n]\n\n\ndef workflow_default(c: Composition, parser: WorkflowArgumentParser) -&gt; None:\n    ",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./test/cloudtest/test_replica_restart.py",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 95,
            "column": 74
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 175
          }
        },
        "contents": "# Test that a crashed (and restarted) cluster replica generates expected notice\n# events.\ndef test_crash_clusterd(mz: MaterializeApplication) -&gt; None:\n    mz.environmentd.sql(\"DROP TABLE IF EXISTS t1 CASCADE\")\n    mz.environmentd.sql(\"CREATE TABLE t1 (f1 TEXT)\")\n\n    # For various query contexts, create a connection, run a query that'll never\n    # finish in another thread, and examine its notices from this thread since\n    # the queries block forever. The contexts here (SELECT stuck in pending,\n    # direct SUBSCRIBE, SUBSCRIBE via COPY) are all separately implemented, so\n    # need to be separately tested.\n    c_select = mz.environmentd.sql_conn()\n    t_select = threading.Thread(\n        target=query,\n        args=(\n            c_select,\n            \"SELECT * FROM t1 AS OF 18446744073709551615\",\n        ),\n    )\n    t_select.start()\n\n    c_subscribe = mz.environmentd.sql_conn()\n    t_subscribe = threading.Thread(\n        target=query,\n        args=(\n            c_subscribe,\n            \"SUBSCRIBE t1\",\n        ),\n    )\n    t_subscribe.start()\n\n    c_copy = mz.environmentd.sql_conn()\n    t_copy = threading.Thread(\n        target=copy,\n        args=(\n            c_copy,\n            \"COPY (SUBSCRIBE t1) TO STDOUT\",\n        ),\n    )\n    t_copy.start()\n\n    # Wait a teeny bit for the queries to be receiving notices.\n    time.sleep(1)\n\n    c_select.notices.clear()\n    c_subscribe.notices.clear()\n    c_copy.notices.clear()\n\n    # Simulate an unexpected clusterd crash.\n    pods = mz.kubectl(\"get\", \"pods\", \"-o\", \"custom-columns=:metadata.name\")\n    podcount = 0\n    for pod in pods.splitlines():\n        if \"cluster\" in pod:\n            try:\n                mz.kubectl(\"delete\", \"pod\", pod)\n                podcount += 1\n            except:\n                # It's OK if the pod delete fails --\n                # it probably means we raced with a previous test that\n                # dropped resources.\n                pass\n    assert podcount &gt; 0\n\n    # Wait for expected notices on all connections.\n    msg",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "# Test that a crashed (and restarted) cluster replica generates expected notice",
            "lineNumber": 99
          },
          {
            "text": "# events.",
            "lineNumber": 100
          },
          {
            "text": "def test_crash_clusterd(mz: MaterializeApplication) -&gt; None:",
            "lineNumber": 101,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "    mz.environmentd.sql(\"DROP TABLE IF EXISTS t1 CASCADE\")",
            "lineNumber": 102
          },
          {
            "text": "    mz.environmentd.sql(\"CREATE TABLE t1 (f1 TEXT)\")",
            "lineNumber": 103
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 104
          },
          {
            "text": "    # For various query contexts, create a connection, run a query that'll never",
            "lineNumber": 105
          },
          {
            "text": "    # finish in another thread, and examine its notices from this thread since",
            "lineNumber": 106
          },
          {
            "text": "    # the queries block forever. The contexts here (SELECT stuck in pending,",
            "lineNumber": 107
          },
          {
            "text": "    # direct SUBSCRIBE, SUBSCRIBE via COPY) are all separately implemented, so",
            "lineNumber": 108
          },
          {
            "text": "    # need to be separately tested.",
            "lineNumber": 109

        "contents": "    /// Details: &lt;https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_roles_create_for-user_externalid.html&gt;\n    #[clap(long, env = \"AWS_EXTERNAL_ID\", value_name = \"ID\", value_parser = AwsExternalIdPrefix::new_from_cli_argument_or_environment_variable)]\n    aws_external_id_prefix: Option&lt;AwsExternalIdPrefix&gt;,\n\n    /// The ARN for a Materialize-controlled role to assume before assuming\n    /// a customer's requested role for an AWS connection.\n    #[clap(long, env = \"AWS_CONNECTION_ROLE_ARN\")]\n    aws_connection_role_arn: Option&lt;String&gt;,\n\n    // === Secrets reader options. ===\n    #[clap(flatten)]\n    secrets: SecretsReaderCliArgs,\n\n    // === Tracing options. ===\n    #[clap(flatten)]\n    tracing: TracingCliArgs,\n\n    // === Other options. ===\n    /// An opaque identifier for the environment in which this process is\n    /// running.\n    #[clap(long, env = \"ENVIRONMENT_ID\")]\n    environment_id: String,\n\n    /// A scratch directory that can be used for ephemeral storage.\n    #[clap(long, env = \"SCRATCH_DIRECTORY\", value_name = \"PATH\")]\n    scratch_directory: Option&lt;PathBuf&gt;,\n\n    /// Memory limit (bytes) of the cluster replica, if known.\n    ///\n    /// The limit is expected to be enforced by the orchestrator. The clusterd process only uses it\n    /// to inform configuration of backpressure mechanism.\n    #[clap(long)]\n    announce_memory_limit: Option&lt;usize&gt;,\n\n    /// Heap limit (bytes) of the cluster replica.\n    ///\n    /// A process heap usage is calculated as the sum of its memory and swap usage.\n    ///\n    /// In contrast to &#96;announce_memory_limit&#96;, this limit is enforced by the clusterd process. If\n    /// the limit is exceeded, the process terminates itself with a 167 exit code.\n    #[clap(long)]\n    heap_limit: Option&lt;usize&gt;,\n\n    /// Whether this size represents a modern \"cc\" size rather than a legacy\n    /// T-shirt size.\n    #[clap(long)]\n    is_cc: bool,\n\n    /// Set core affinity for Timely workers.\n    ///\n    /// This flag should only be set if the process is provided with exclusive access to its\n    /// supplied CPU cores. I have the full picture. The flag is `--orchestrator-process-propagate-crashes`. When enabled, the process orchestrator will crash the parent (environmentd) when a child process (like clusterd) crashes. The env var is `ORCHESTRATOR_PROCESS_PROPAGATE_CRASHES`.

---


Use this so environmentd exits when a child (e.g. clusterd) crashes:

**Flag:** `--orchestrator-process-propagate-crashes`

**Env var:** `ORCHESTRATOR_PROCESS_PROPAGATE_CRASHES` (set to a truthy value, e.g. `1`)

From `src/environmentd/src/environmentd/main.rs`:

```268:271:src/environmentd/src/environmentd/main.rs
    /// Whether the process orchestrator should handle crashes in child
    /// processes by crashing the parent process.
    #[clap(long, env = "ORCHESTRATOR_PROCESS_PROPAGATE_CRASHES")]
    orchestrator_process_propagate_crashes: bool,
```

This is only used when the **process** orchestrator is in use (e.g. `mz run environmentd` / `--orchestrator=process`). In the orchestrator, when a child crashes and `propagate_crashes` is true, it aborts with a message like “crashed; aborting because propagate_crashes is enabled”.

**Example:**

```bash
environmentd --orchestrator-process-propagate-crashes ...
# or
ORCHESTRATOR_PROCESS_PROPAGATE_CRASHES=1 environmentd ...
```

So `bin/environmentd` is a script that does:

exec "$(dirname "$0")"/run environmentd "$@"

So it calls `run environmentd` with all the arguments passed through (`$@`). The `run` script is likely the Python `misc/python/materialize/cli/run.py` we saw earlier. So when you run `bin/environmentd --orchestrator-process-propagate-crashes`, it becomes `run environmentd --orchestrator-process-propagate-crashes`.
